observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Accountant complies with the pertinent provisions of the IPSAS 1 by recording the Municipality’s Due to NGAs account in adherence with the requirement of fair presentation of the accounts and adequately disclosing relevant information in the Notes to FS for these to be fairly presented. We recommend that the Municipal Accountant maintain a complete subsidiary ledger for every fund transfer. We also recommend that the Local Chief Executive direct the officials concerned to evaluate and review the existing fund balances in order to determine the proper disposition of the funds and return the excess fund to the source agencies.
observations.reason: The Municipal Accountant has yet to disclose in the financial statements the necessary details of the Due to NGAs account

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Accountant revisit the Trust Fund books to determine the transactions that brought about the Government Equity balance and effect the necessary adjustments. Henceforth, observe the proper recording of transactions in the Trust Fund books, and the preparation of its financial statements.

We recommend the Special Disbursing Officer to abide by the general guidelines and documentary requirements in the release, use, and liquidation of confidential and intelligence funds; prepare and submit to ICFAU the Liquidation Report of cash advances supported with documentary requirements; and the Municipal Accountant to ensure that all cash advances for CF/IF have complete supporting documents, refrain from granting additional cash advances unless the previous ones were already settled and/or liquidated, and ensure that the cash advance voucher with covering transmittal letter enumerating the supporting documents be submitted within seven (7) working days after the release of the check, to the COA ATL for post audit.
observations.reason: The Municipal Accountant has yet to be furnished with the certified list of the real property taxpayers.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that: a) The Municipal Accountant establish RPT and SET Receivables at the beginning of the year based on the certified list of real property taxpayers and the amount due and collectible for the year; and record collection of taxes in accordance with Section 20 of NGAS; and b) The Municipal Treasurer regularly update and furnish the Municipal Accountant at the beginning of the year with a certified list of real property taxpayers with the collectible amounts to serve as basis for the recording of receivables and enforce collection of unpaid delinquent taxes through tax campaigns.
observations.reason: The MA has to be furnished yet with the certified list of real property taxpayers

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Accountant prepare the appropriate journal entries to correctly classify the liabilities incurred by the Municipality in the financial statements and make necessary adjustments to the books in order to facilitate a fair presentation on the financial statements.
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Accountant to verify the existence of the balances of the account and make necessary adjustments to the books, if necessary.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant exert effort to properly verify the validity of the Liability accounts, and make necessary adjustments to the book in order to facilitate a fair presentation on the financial statements.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountant, Municipal Treasurer, and other personnel concerned to gather pertinent supporting documents and update and reconcile the balances in their books with that of the DILG and prepare fund utilization reports accordingly.
observations.reason: Documents needed to facilitate the liquidation of fund is still not available.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Accountant be required to submit disbursement vouchers and supporting documents with complete relevant supporting documents and to observe deadlines set for submission of accounts and other financial reports as required under relevant provisions; and to suspend the payment of the salaries of the responsible employees for the delayed and non-submission of required reports until these have been complied. Impose applicable administrative and penal sanctions against erring officials if warranted.
observations.reason: There are still eight (8) DVs that are unsubmitted with the lacking details.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the concerned municipal officials to prepare the necessary records and reports to determine whether the project generate income and its effectiveness; and determine whether the project generate income.
observations.reason: The Municipality did not submit a summary of collections for the water system against the loan amortization to determine whether the project generate income.

observations.recommendation: We recommended to submit immediately the disbursement vouchers and its supporting documents to avoid issuance of Notice of Suspension and Disallowance and impose applicable administrative and penal sanctions as warranted.
observations.reason: There are still unsubmitted DVs amounting to P2,545,487.06.

observations.recommendation: Require the former Mayor to immediately submit the lacking supporting documents to the Audit Team and Liquidation Report to the COA Chairman in a sealed envelope and furnish the Audit Team. Henceforth management should strictly follow the provisions of COA Circular No. 2003-003 and COA Circular No. 2012-001.
observations.reason: The said cash advances are still outstanding. The LGU already sent a Demand Letter to the Former Mayor.

observations.recommendation:
1.7. We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to revisit the cause
of the reported negative cash balance and henceforth ensure that all approved
claims shall be supported by sufficient funds availability.

1.8. We further recommended and the Acting Municipal Treasurer agreed to
endeavor further strategies to raise additional revenues due to the
Municipalities.

3.11. We recommended and the Supply Officer agreed to:

c. conduct a complete inventory of all properties owned by the LGU and
submit the same to the Audit Team not later than January 31 of each year;
and

d. continuously reconcile its records with the Office of the Municipal
Accountant.

3.12. We also recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to prepare
journal entries to correct the deficiencies noted.

3.13. Furthermore, we recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to
spearhead efforts to comply with the procedural guidelines of COA Circular
2020-006 on the one-time cleansing of PPE accounts in order for the Municipal
Government present fairly its financial statements.

4.18. We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to:

e. determine the nature/causes of the non-moving, undocumented, and
negative balances and prepare the necessary adjustments to come up with
reconciled and correct account balances;

f. provide sufficient supporting documents to adjustments taken up in the
books, and henceforth submit the same together as attachments to
approved JEVs on a monthly basis;

g. record the financial transactions in accordance with the Chart of
Accounts; and

h. adhere to the requirement of fair presentation of accounts by adequately
disclosing relevant information in the Notes to FS.

5.7. We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to take up the
current portion of Loans Payable – Domestic under current liabilities to
present fairly the financial statements of the Municipal Government in
accordance with the pertinent provisions of the International Public Sector
Accounting Standards 1.

6.10. We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to:

d. prepare the necessary adjusting entries to reflect the correct balance of the
Due to BIR account;

e. file an application for a tax refund or request for the over-remitted
amount to be credited against future tax liabilities; and

f. henceforth, ensure that proper reconciliation was made to verify
withholding and remittance amounts before filing and payment to the BIR;
and
Moreover, we recommended and the Municipal Treasurer agreed to remit the
tax deficiencies noted on the taxes withheld from the suppliers for CY 2024.

7.9. We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to:

a. Make the necessary adjusting entries to correct the erroneous recognition
of the cost of improvements and major repairs to the specific PPE account
instead of Repairs and Maintenance Expense pursuant to IPAS 17, and
provide and compute the related depreciation expense of the building.

b. Strictly adhere to IPAS 17 on capitalization of assets for proper reporting
of the balances in the Financial Statement

8.12. We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to make the
necessary adjusting entries and, henceforth capitalize the borrowing costs to
the related asset account until the completion of the projects financed through
loans:
Construction in Progress xxx
Prior Period Adjustment xxx

9.12. We recommended and:

a. The Human Resource Management Officer and other concerned officials
agreed to ensure that entries in the DTRs reflect the actual work schedules
of the JO and COS personnel to ensure the reliability and validity of the
documents.

b. The Municipal Accountant agreed to ensure the completeness of
supporting documents before claims are processed as provided in COA
Circular No. 2012-001.

c. Management agreed to strictly adhere to the policies and guidelines in the
hiring of personnel under contract of services and/or job orders as
provided for in CSC Resolution No. 02-0790 dated June 5, 2002.

12.7. We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to direct the LSB to
immediately post the CY 2024 SEF utilization reports in at least three (3)
conspicuous public places. We recommend, thereafter, to direct the LSB to
faithfully comply with the full disclosure policy of the DILG in compliance with
Section 6 of DepEd-DBM-DILG JC No. 2017-1.

13.15. We recommended and the GAD Focal Point System agreed to:

a. initiate the conduct of gender analysis and thereafter prepare the GAD
Agenda of the Municipal Government based on the identified gender gaps
or issues therein;

b. ensure compliance with the established submission timelines of the GPB
and GAD AR as outlined in COA Circular No. 2014-001; and

c. assign the appropriate Responsibility Center (RC) for GAD to ensure
proper tracking, monitoring, and reporting of gender-related
expenditures, in accordance with the procedures and guidelines set forth
in COA Circular No. 2021-008.

We recommend that the Municipal Accountant to: a. reclassify all completed projects still carried in the Construction in Progress accounts to the appropriate asset accounts based on the Certificates of Completion issued by the Office of the Municipal Engineer; and b. prepare journal entries to correct the deficiencies noted. Moreover, we recommend that the Supply Officer to: a. conduct complete inventory of all properties owned by the LGU and submit the same to the Audit Team not later than January 31 of each year; and b. indicate proper classification of PPE in the RPCPPE. We also recommend that the Local Chief Executive: a. direct the Municipal Accountant and the Supply Officer to reconcile the balances per book with that of the inventory report and to set up and maintain PPE ledgers cards and property cards for all PPE items; and b. direct the officers concerned to observe COA Circular No. 2015-008 so that proper disclosures of the road network account in the FS will be achieved.
observations.reason: The Supply Officer to: a. conduct complete inventory of all properties owned by the LGU and submit the same to the Audit Team not later than January 31 of each year; and b. indicate proper classification of PPE in the RPCPPE.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Accountant immediately effect the necessary adjustments/corrections in the books upon verification, transfer the overdue amounts to the appropriate funds, and henceforth conduct periodic reconciliations of the reciprocal accounts to fairly present the financial transactions and accounts of the Municipality.

We recommend that Municipal Accountant make the necessary adjusting journal entries to recognize the unliquidated Cash Advances under the Confidential Fund for CY 2023 and prior years, if any. Moreover, subsequent releases from the Confidential Fund should be initially recorded as Advances to Special Disbursing Officer, and only be considered liquidated upon receipt of the copy of the transmittal letter of liquidation, duly acknowledged by the ICFAU. We also recommend the Municipal Accountant to ensure that all cash advances for CF/IF have complete supporting documents and to prepare and submit the Liquidation Report of cash advances supported with documentary requirements in accordance with the aforementioned Joint Circular.

We recommend the Municipal Budget Officer ensure that savings from the approved Bids funds were duly approved by the Sanggunian before certifying any availability of allotment for honoraria chargeable to the General Fund (GF); and the Municipal Accountant strengthen control on the disbursement of funds by disapproving payments for BAC honoraria which are not supported with documents and not properly funded, in consonance with COA Circular No. 2012-001, DBM BC Nos. 2007-3 and 2004-5A.

We recommend that the Local Chief Executive ensure the implementation of PPAs not yet implemented in CY 2022, and subsequently spearhead the appropriate planning in identifying priority development projects to ensure PPAs are well-planned and procurement/implementation-ready as mandated by laws and regulations.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive requiring the Municipal Accountant, Municipal Treasurer, and other personnel concerned to gather pertinent supporting documents and update and reconcile the balances in their books with that of the DILG and prepare fund utilization reports accordingly. Also recommended that the Municipal Accountant effect the necessary adjustments/corrections in the books; recognize obligation when goods are delivered or services rendered; and henceforth require the submission of other relevant documents to substantiate the setting-up of Accounts Payable at year-end.

We recommend that the MDRRMO include the unexpended prior years’ balances in the LDRRMFIP to ensure preparedness for natural and man-made hazards and that the Municipal Accountant transfer all unexpended balances of LDRRMF excluding continuing appropriations to the TF bank account.

We recommend that the Local Chief Executive ensure the implementation of PPAs not yet implemented, and subsequently spearhead the appropriate planning of priority development projects to come up with PPAs that are well-planned and procurement/implementation-ready as mandated by laws and regulations.

We recommend that the LDRRMO include in the LDRRMFIP the list of programs, projects, and activities charged to the prior year’s unexpended LDRRMF in the ensuing year in accordance with COA Circular No. 2012-001.

a) The Municipal Accountant to: reclassify all completed projects still carried in the Construction in Progress accounts to the appropriate asset accounts based on the Certificates of Completion issued by the Office of the Municipal Engineer; and prepare journal entries to correct the deficiencies noted.
Moreover, we recommended that the Supply Officer to: conduct a complete inventory of all properties owned by the LGU and submit the same to the Audit Team not later than January 31 of each year; indicate proper account classification of PPE in the RPCPPE; and continuously reconcile its records with the Office of the Municipal Accountant.
We also recommended that the management initiate the necessary procedures to cleanse its PPE account to achieve a fair presentation of the account.
observations.reason: Reiterated in this report

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Accountant make the necessary adjustments to properly classify and record payables and other liabilities and ensure fair presentation in the financial statements.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant maintain a complete subsidiary ledger for every fund transfer and adequately disclose in the face of the Statement of Financial Position or in the Notes the necessary details of the Due to NGAs account to provide users with additional information that is useful in relevant decision-making. We also recommend that the Local Chief Executive direct the officials concerned to evaluate and review the existing fund balances in order to determine the proper disposition of the funds and return the excess fund to the source agencies.

We recommend that the Local Chief Executive as head of the Municipal Development Council and all personnel concerned to:
a. ensure that the proposed PPAs are properly itemized in the Annual Investment Plan (AIP) and the related information such as the specific timeline and expected output are defined in the AIP to facilitate monitoring and evaluation; and
b. effect appropriate planning in identifying priority development projects to ensure that PPAs are well-planned and procurement and implementation-ready as mandated by laws and regulations.
observations.reason: Reiterated in this report

observations.recommendation: We reiterated that the MDRRMO includes all the unexpended prior years’ balances in the LDRRMFIP to ensure the Municipality’s preparedness for natural and man-made hazards or disasters. We also recommend that the Municipal Accountant transfer all unexpended balances of LDRRMF excluding continuing appropriations to the TF bank account.

We recommended that the Local Chief facilitate the liquidation of fund transfers from DILG RO VIII, and direct Municipal Accountant, Municipal Treasurer, and other personnel concerned to gather pertinent supporting documents and update and reconcile the balances in their books with that of the DILG and prepare fund utilization reports accordingly.
Impose the applicable administrative and penal sanctions provided for under the cited regulations against the erring officials if warranted under circumstances.

observations.recommendation: The said cash advances totaling P165,000.00 granted despite inadequate supporting documents and without first liquidating the previous cash advances should be liquidated and supporting documents submitted.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant to:
a. reclassify all completed projects still carried in the Construction in
Progress accounts to the appropriate asset accounts based on the
Certificates of Completion issued by the Office of the Municipal Engineer;
and
b. prepare journal entries to correct the deficiencies noted.
Moreover, we recommended that the Supply Officer to:
a. conduct a complete inventory of all properties owned by the LGU and
submit the same to the Audit Team not later than January 31 of each year;
b. indicate proper account classification of PPE in the RPCPPE; and
c. continuously reconcile its records with the Office of the Municipal
Accountant.
We also recommended that the management initiate the necessary procedures
to cleanse its PPE account to achieve a fair presentation of the account.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant complies with the pertinent
provisions of the IPSAS 1 by recording the Municipality’s Due to NGAs
account balance in adherence with the requirement of fair presentation of
accounts and adequately disclosing relevant information in the Notes to FS for
these to be fairly presented.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant immediately effect the
necessary adjustments/corrections in the books upon verification, transfer the
overdue amounts to the appropriate funds, and henceforth conduct periodic
reconciliations of the reciprocal accounts to fairly present the financial
transactions and accounts of the Municipality.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant make the necessary
adjusting journal entries to recognize the unliquidated Cash Advances under
the Confidential Fund for CY 2023 and prior years, if any.

We also recommended that subsequent releases from the Confidential Fund
be initially recorded as Advances to Special Disbursing Officer, and only
consider it as liquidated upon receipt of the copy of the transmittal letter of
liquidation, duly acknowledged by the ICFAU.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant revisit the Trust Fund books
to determine the transactions that brought about the Government Equity
balance and effect the necessary adjustments. Henceforth, observe the proper
recording of transactions in the Trust Fund books, and the preparation of its
financial statements.

We recommended that the:
a. Municipal Budget Officer ensure that savings from the approved local
budget were duly approved by the local Sanggunian before certifying any
availability of allotment for honoraria chargeable to the General Fund
(GF); and
b. Municipal Accountant strengthen control on the disbursement of funds by
disapproving payments for BAC honoraria which are not supported with
documents and not properly funded, in consonance with the pertinent
provisions of COA Circular No. 2012-001 DBM BC Nos. 2007-3 and 2004-
5A.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive as head of the Municipal
Development Council and all personnel concerned to:
a. ensure that the proposed PPAs are properly itemized in the Annual
Investment Plan (AIP) and the related information such as the specific
timeline and expected output are defined in the AIP to facilitate monitoring
and evaluation; and
b. effect appropriate planning in identifying priority development projects to
ensure that PPAs are well-planned and procurement and implementation-
ready as mandated by laws and regulations.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the:
a. Special Disbursing Officer (SDO) to –
a. abide by the general guidelines and documentary requirements in the
release, use, and liquidation of confidential and intelligence funds; and
b. prepare and submit to ICFAU, the Liquidation Report of cash
advances supported with documentary requirements in accordance
with the abovementioned Joint Circular; and
b. Municipal Accountant to ensure that all cash advances for CF/IF have
complete supporting documents, refrain from granting additional cash
advances unless the previous ones were already settled and/or liquidated,
and see to it that the cash advance voucher with covering transmittal letter
enumerating the supporting documents be submitted within seven (7)
working days after the release of the check, to the COA ATL for post
audit.

We recommended that the Management submits justifications for allowing
partial/delayed delivery of the prepositioned goods and the reason for the
inconsistencies on the dates of receipt/delivery; and
We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant:
a. effect the necessary adjustments/corrections in the books;
b. recognize obligation when goods are delivered or services are rendered;
and
c. henceforth require the submission of other relevant documents to
substantiate the setting-up of Accounts Payable at year-end.

We reiterated that the MDRRMO includes all the unexpended prior years’
balances in the LDRRMFIP to ensure the Municipality’s preparedness for
natural and man-made hazards or disasters. We also recommend that the
Municipal Accountant transfer all unexpended balances of LDRRMF
excluding continuing appropriations to the TF bank account.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant make the necessary adjusting entries to the affected accounts, and properly record the succeeding loan amortization and provide necessary disclosure in the Notes to Financial Statement.

We recommend:
a) prepare the Job Order Memorandum and Contract of Service consistent with the CSC requirements under Rule XI of CSC Memorandum No. 40, series of 1998 as amended by SCS Memorandum No. 15, series of 1999; b) review and ensure that the number of days worked indicated in the DTR tallies with the actual work rendition; c) submit the original/authenticated copies of logbook/biometric printouts (whichever is applicable) as supporting documents for the payment of their wages; and d) assess its existing organizational structure so that reasonable plantilla personnel will be established in order to fulfill its mandate of providing basic services.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive identify programs/projects/activities that will strengthen the capacity of the LGU for disaster risk reduction and management and ensure the full implementation of the PPAs within the timeframe to optimize the utilization of the fund.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Accountant to verify the existence of the balances of the account and make necessary adjustments in the books, if necessary. We also recommended to require the accountable officers concerned to liquidate their cash advances pursuant to COA Circular No. 97-002.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare the necessary journal entries to reclassify completed projects from Construction in Progress to appropriate asset accounts and record depreciation, and to cleanse CIP and PPE balances and properly disclose adjustments.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountant, Inventory Committee and other concerned personnel to initiate necessary steps in the turnover of completed barangay infrastructure projects located within the territorial jurisdiction of barangays in order to transfer the responsibility in managing and maintaining the properties.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant exert effort to properly verify the validity of Financial Liability accounts, and make necessary adjustments to the book prepared in order to facilitate a fair presentation on the financial statements.
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare the necessary adjusting journal entries to reflect the correct balance of the accounts in the Financial Statement.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct LDRRMC, MBO and other concerned officials to conduct a review of the balances of continuing appropriation, especially those that are outstanding for more than 5 years already, to determine appropriate action of the fund in accordance with RA7160. We also recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the transfer of the amount corresponding to the unspent LDRRMF from the General Fund to Trust Fund and the Municipal Accountant prepare the necessary journal entries to correct the balance of the Trust Liabilities – DRRMF account as suggested in Section 5.1.13 and Annex C of COA Circular No. 2012-002.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require:
a. the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Budget Officer to (a) comply with the provisions of PPSAS 24 on the preparation of Statement of Comparison of Budget and Actual Amounts, and (b) reconcile their records on allotments and obligations monthly and monthly so that any resulting discrepancies can be immediately verified and corrected; b. the Municipal Accountant to provide disclosures on the face of the SCBAA and/or in the Notes to FS on the (i) material discrepancies between the budget and actual amounts; and (ii) reconciliation of actual amounts per SCBAA and per financial statements.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Planning Officer and Budget Officer to ensure that AIP should be prepared and approved before the start of the budget preparation and to see to it that the budget shall fund only those PPAs that are included in the AIP.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
a. Direct the Local Budget Officer and Planning Officer to submit additional documents to support the appropriation of the 20% Development Fund into specific PPAs as required under LBC No. 112; b. Spearhead the appropriate planning of priority development projects to ensure the implementation of PPAs and the delivery of services as mandated by laws and regulations.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the MDRRMO and the Municipal Accountant to prepare and submit the monthly Report on the Sources and Utilization of DRRMF following the format on Annex B of COA Circular No. 2012-002. We also recommended that the Municipal Administrator be instructed that effective CY 2022, to fully disclose in the Notes to Financial Statement the details of the LDRRMF following the format on Annex D of COA Circular No. 2012-002.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
1. Require the General Services Officer to issue the Inventory Custodian Slip (ICS) to end-user of the various inventories amounting to P1,660,987.08. Also, to conduct physical count of all inventories previously issued and maintain the stock cards for each inventory items found on hand. Further, prepare and submit the Report on the Physical Count of Inventories to the Audit Team.

We recommended that the Municipal Local Chief Executive instruct municipal officials concerned to strictly comply the regulations on the utilization and reporting of LDRRMF in order to ascertain the legality of transactions and promote transparency on LDRRMF.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the LDC and MPD Office to ensure that all programs, projects, and activities of the LGU are verified in the Annual Investment Program and supported with any revised AIP in order to be certain on propriety of the projects implemented by the LGU.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Treasurer to verify the Official Receipts issued by contractors in payment of infrastructure projects and ensure that the information reflected therein are sufficient as required in Section 113 of RA No. 8424, before the release of checks for payment in order not to deprive the Government of the correct taxes due from the contractors.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct personnel concerned to strictly follow the provisions of COA Circular No. 97-002 and PD 1445 on the proper granting, utilization and liquidation of cash advances and that no additional cash advances shall be allowed to any official or employee unless the previous cash advance given to him is first settled or a proper accounting thereof is made. Demand letters should be sent to officials and employees with outstanding balances to settle their cash advances.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct personnel concerned to strictly comply with Section 5.1.5 of COA Circular dated September 12, 2012 on the submission of the Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF to COA.

1.6 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal
Accountant to verify the existence of the balances of the account and make necessary
adjustments in the books, if necessary.

1.7 We also recommended to require the accountable officers concerned to
liquidate their cash advances pursuant to COA Circular No. 97-002.

2.5 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal
Accountant to draw a JEV for the transfer of completed projects to their appropriate
Property Plant and Equipment account and make the necessary adjustments relative
to the current and prior year’s unrecorded depreciation expense.

3.6 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal
Accountant, Inventory Committee, and other concerned personnel to initiate
necessary steps in the turnover of completed barangay infrastructure projects located
within the territorial jurisdiction of barangays in order to transfer the responsibility
in managing and maintaining the properties.

3.7 We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare the adjusting
entries to account for the transfer made to the Barangays.

4.5 We recommended that the Municipal Accountant exert effort to properly
verify the validity of Financial Liability accounts, and make necessary adjustments
to the book in order to facilitate a fair presentation on the financial statements.

5.5 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal
Accountant to exert effort in the determination of the composition of the accounts
Due to NGAs and Due to LGUs and prepare a supporting schedule and a Subsidiary
Ledger to support the balances reported in General Ledger.

6.5 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal
Accountant to prepare the necessary adjusting journal entries to reflect the correct
balance of the accounts in the Financial Statement.

6.6 The Municipal Accountant concurred with the observation and correcting entries
were already prepared.

7.6 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal
Accountant, Municipal Treasurer, and other personnel concerned to gather pertinent
supporting documents and update and reconcile the balances in their books with that
of the DILG and prepare fund utilization reports accordingly.

8.7 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the LDRRMC, MBO
and other concerned officials to conduct a review of the balances of continuing
appropriation, especially those that are outstanding for more than 5 years already, to
determine appropriate action of the fund in accordance with Section 322 of RA7160.

8.8 We also recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the transfer of the
amount corresponding to the unspent LDRRMF from the General Fund to Trust
Fund and the Municipal Accountant prepare the necessary journal entries to correct
the balance of the Trust Liabilities – DRRMF account as suggested in Section 5.1.13
and Annex C of COA Circular No. 2012-002.

9.7 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Planning Officer and the Budget Officer to ensure that AIP should be prepared and approved before the start of the budget preparation and to see to it that the budget shall fund only those PPAs that are included in the AIP.

10.13 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:

a. Direct the Local Budget Officer and Planning Officer to submit additional
documents to support the appropriation of the 20% Development Fund
into specific PPAs as required under LBC No. 112
b. Spearheads the appropriate planning of priority development projects to
ensure the implementation of PPAs and the delivery of services as
mandated by laws and regulations.

11.6 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the LDRRMO to
prepare the LDRRMFIP in the ensuing year in accordance with COA Circular No.
2012-001.

12.6 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the MDRRMO and
the Municipal Accountant to prepare and submit a monthly Report on the Sources
and Utilization of DRRMF following the format on Annex B of COA Circular No.
2012-002.

12.7 We also recommended the Municipal Accountant be instructed that effective
CY 2022, to fully disclosed in the Notes to Financial Statement the details of the
LDRRMF following the format on Annex D of COA Circular No. 2012-002.

1.7 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal
Accountant to properly record, classify and disclose the account balances
affected in the financial statements in order to present fairly the accounts.

2.8 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
a. Direct the Municipal Accountant to make necessary adjustments on the
proper recognition of the BGCM and also, adjust the accounts that were
directly recorded as an expense to its proper assets accounts.
b. Require the General Services Officer to issue the Inventory Custodian Slip
(ICS) to end-user of the various inventories amounting to P1,660,987.08. Also,
to conduct physical count of all inventories previously issued and maintain
the stock cards for each inventory items found on hand. Further, prepare and
submit the Report on the Physical Count of Inventories to the Municipal
Accountant as basis for recording.
c. Prepare and submit the monthly Report on Fund Utilization and Status of
Program/Project/Activity Implementation in accordance with Annex C of
DBM LBC No. 125, in coordination with other local officials concerned such
as the Municipal Accountant.
d. Complete the necessary supporting documents in accordance with the
provisions of GPPB Circular No. 01-2020 to avoid suspensions or
disallowances in audit.

3.5 We recommended and the municipal officials concerned agreed to strictly
comply the regulations on the utilization and reporting of LDRRMF in order to
ascertain the legality of transactions and promote transparency on LDRRMF.

4.5 We recommended and the LDC and MPD Office agreed to ensure that all
programs, projects, and activities of the LGU are verified in the Annual
Investment Program and supported with any revised AIP in order to be certain
on the propriety of the projects implemented by the LGU.

5.5 We recommended and the ICO-Municipal Treasurer agreed to verify the
Official Receipts issued by contractors in payment of infrastructure projects
and ensure that the information reflected therein are sufficient as required in
Section 113 of RA No. 8424, before the release of checks for payment in order
not to deprive the Government of the correct taxes due from the contractors.

6.8 We recommended and the municipal officials concerned agreed to submit
supporting documents and comply with the posting requirements for the
disbursments made under the fund in order to not affect the validity of the
transaction.

We  recommended  that  the  Municipal  Mayor  direct   the   Municipal
 Accountant to prepare the necessary adjusting entries to  correct  the
 understatement  of  P1,505,420.20  in   the   GF.   Correctly   record
 transactions in order to fairly present the balance of the account.


 We also recommended that the  Municipal  Mayor  direct  the  Municipal
 Accountant  to  record  bank  reconciling  items  with  the  necessary
 documents that will support the transactions in  order  to  present  a
 reliable balance of Cash in Bank – LCCA and to not affect  pervasively
 the amounts recorded in the financial statements. Restore  the  amount
 transferred to Other Losses account back to Cash-in-Bank, LCCA account
 as unreconciled items and, adjustments shall only  be  made  if  these
 were already cleared or reconciled.

We recommended that the Local Chief  Executive  direct  the  Municipal
 Accountant to properly  record  and  classify  the  account  balances
 affected in the financial statements in order to present  fairly  the
 accounts and not to mislead the users of the report.

We recommended that the Local Chief  Executive  direct  the  Municipal
 Officials  concerned  to  properly   plan,   implement   and   monitor
 implementation of infrastructure projects of the municipality  in  order
 to not hamper the benefits it can give to the constituents.  Further,  we
 also recommended to apply and compute the liquidated damages  on  the
 number of delays on the construction  of  projects  as  penalty to the
 contractor.


      This  is  without  prejudice  to  the  issuance  of  the   Notice   of
 Disallowance due to the delay in the completion of the projects.

We recommended that the Accountant be required to submit immediately the lacking disbursement vouchers with complete relevant supporting documents and to keenly observe the deadline set for the submission of accounts and other financial reports as required under Sections 4, 39, 107 and 122 of P.D. No. 1445 and Sections 7.1.1, 7.1.2, 7.2.1 (a) and (b) of COA Circular 2009-006. We further recommended that Management suspend the payment of the salaries of the responsible employees for the delayed and non-submission of the required reports until these have been complied in pursuance with Section 127 of the same Code.
observations.reason: There were still eight (8) DVs which remained to be unsubmitted.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountant to submit the monthly and quarterly trial balance, financial statements and its accompanying schedules within the period prescribed by the above rules and regulations in order to have a timely verification and analysis of accounts and the immediate submission of the audit results to have a reliable information on the financial condition and results of operations of the municipality.
observations.reason: Reports were still submitted not within the prescribed period.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management shall ensure that the Audit Team be furnished with the copy of the aforementioned contracts immediately and strictly comply with the provisions in COA Circular 2009-001 which provides that contracts shall be submitted within five (5) working days from its execution supported with complete documentary requirements as enumerated in the circular.

We recommended to the Local Chief Executive to require the Municipal Accountant and the HRMO Designate to strictly attach proper and complete documents in the payment of wages of the job orders, and furnish a copy of the Memorandum/Contract of Service to contractual/job order personnel stating the specific work assignments they are expected to deliver, compensation, as well as other terms of the contract between both parties, in order to ascertain the propriety of the payment of the wages. Also, we seek justification or explanation of the 16 job order workers who did not present themselves for validation.

We recommended to the Local Chief Executive and to the concerned personnel to exercise prudence and stop payment of traveling expenses to the contractual and job order workers so that funds can be used for development programs and projects of the municipality.

We recommended to the Local Chief Executive to prepare a contract of service between the municipality and the consultant stipulating the required technical expertise and skills needed for the provision of a consultant and strictly follow RA 9184. We also recommend requiring the political consultant to prepare and submit proper and detailed accomplishment reports in relation to the contract of service in order to support the payment for the services.

We recommended to the Local Chief Executive to strictly follow the above laws, rules and regulations and refrain from giving the roles and functions of the regular personnel to the job order worker. Also, the LCE must determine the proper persons responsible and liable for the position to avoid loss of government funds in the future.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive to enjoin the BuB focal person to fast track the implementation of project and to strictly observe the respective timetables in the implementation. Also we recommend to return any excess amount or unutilized fun to DA-BFAR RO8.
observations.reason: Fish completed. Sanctuaries were still on-going per inquiry to the Municipal Agriculturist.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant must thoroughly review the propriety of a transaction to be charged to the LDRRMF in accordance with Section 5 of NDRRMC-DBM-DILG JMC No. 2013-1, in order to prevent the payment of unrelated expenses that could lead to disallowance in the audit. The MDRRM Officer, through the LDRRMC and LDC, must also render a monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of LDRRMF as certified correct by the Municipal Accountant to the auditor, to facilitate review and monitoring of the fund. The same must also be posted in a conspicuous place within the LGU quarterly. Likewise, the Municipal Accountant must monitor the submission of Disbursement Vouchers and the immediate liquidation of cash advances to ensure that all transactions are duly accounted and reviewed.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive to direct the Municipal Officials concerned to prepare the necessary records and reports to determine whether the project generate income and effectiveness and efficiency have been attained.
observations.reason: There were still no reports prepared referring to the income generating project of the agency.

observations.recommendation: We also recommended that the Local Chief Executive should create an inventory committee consisting of two or more employees including the property custodian who will spearhead the physical count of property, plant and equipment and inventories of supplies and materials, and submit the Inventory Reports to the Commission on Audit for validation and reconciliation to the financial statements in order to ascertain the fair presentation of the accounts. We also recommend that the Municipal Accountant be directed to maintain Supply Ledger Card, Property, Plant and Equipment Ledger Card, while the Supply Officer should also maintain Stock Card and Property Card, he should also prepare and submit to accounting unit the monthly Summary of Supplies and Materials Issued (SSMI) based on the Requisition and Issue Slip (RIS).
observations.reason: There were still no inventory reports submitted to Auditors for the validation and reconciliation to accounts reported by the agency. Furthermore, Supply Ledger Cards, Property, Plant and Equipment Ledger Cards in the Accounting Department and Stock Cards and Property Cards by the Supply Officer were not maintained. Also, no monthly Summary of Supplies and Materials Issued (SSMI) were submitted monthly to the Accounting Department.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor directs the Municipal Accountant to properly record and reconcile the cash and cash equivalents following the prescribed accounting rules and Philippine Public Sector Accounting Standards (PPSAS) on the presentation of accounts in the financial statements in order not to mislead the user on the honest and reliable information on the financial position of the municipality.
observations.reason: Bank reconciliation statements still includes unreconciled amounts.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Accountant should record the interest expense for the loan granted and strictly follow the stated provision in preparation of the financial statements in order to reflect the accuracy and reliability and to achieve fair presentation of the accounts presented.

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor directs the Municipal Accountant concerned to strictly follow the proper granting, utilization and liquidation of cash advances, no additional cash advances shall be allowed to any official or employee unless the previous cash advance given to him is first settled or a proper accounting thereof is made to avoid exposure of funds for misuse and misappropriation and to ascertain the propriety and validity of the transactions. Likewise, demand letters should be sent to officials and employees with outstanding balances to settle their cash advances.
observations.reason: Cash advances were still granted to employees with previous unliquidated advances.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the municipal accountant should strictly follow the prescribed accounting rules and regulations and Philippine Public Sector Accounting Standards (PPSAS) on the presentation of accounts in the financial statements in order not to mislead the user on the honest and reliable information on the financial condition, performance, and equity of the municipality.

Direct the MDRRM Officer, through the LDRRMC and LDC, to render a monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of LDRRMF as certified correct by the Municipal Accountant to the auditor, to facilitate review and monitoring of the fund. Likewise, direct Municipal Accountant to monitor the submission of Disbursement Vouchers and the immediate liquidation of cash advances to ensure that all transactions are duly accounted and reviewed. Furthermore, the Local Chief Executive thru the LDRRMC must initiate the transfer of the unexpended balance of LDRRMF to a Special Trust Account in accordance with the approved regulations pertaining to the utilization and reporting of the fund.

We recommend that the Local Chief Executive direct municipal officials concerned to properly apportion an amount in the Annual Budget corresponding to one percent (1%) of the Internal Revenue Allotment (IRA) for Local Council for Protection of Children in order to support the proper implementation of its programs, projects, and activities.

We recommend that the School Board, headed by the Local Chief Executive, must follow the steps in the planning and approval of SEF budget as specified by law and an approved regulation in order that benefits be duly received by the intended beneficiaries. Also, the Municipal Mayor, Municipal Budget Officer, Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer should approve payment of transactions only if those are within the bounds of an approved budget to prevent misuse of funds and a disallowance in audit.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to immediately submit the said checks, disbursement vouchers as well as the original copies of cancelled checks in accordance with the provision of Sections 7.1 and 7.2 of COA Circular No. 2009-06.
observations.reason: Some vouchers were not found due to typhoon Yolanda and Ruby.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Accountant comply strictly all the documentary requirements in the transfer, implementation and liquidation by the Implementing Agency (IA) and the Source Agency (SA) pursuant to Sections 3.1.1 to 3.1.3 of COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012 in order to achieve properly and timely evaluation of the intended projects, programs and activities charged to transferred funds.
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant ensure that all Fund Transfers are provided by a Memorandum of Agreement for us to verify the intention/purpose of the said transfer.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct personnel concerned to strictly comply with Section 5.1.5 of COA Circular 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012 on the submission of the Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF to further strengthen the capabilities of the LGU in addressing Disaster Risk Reduction Management through regular monitoring of its PPAs.
observations.reason: For CY 2019, the agency submitted monthly utilization report from Jan. to Sept. 2019 only.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive, through its Solid Waste Management Board comply strictly with all the provisions of Republic Act No. 9003 in order to achieve the intended purpose of the law, specifically on the preparation and proper execution of a doable Local Solid Waste Management 10-Year Plan.
observations.reason: The Agency formulated its draft Solid Waste Management Plan and is still waiting for the approval of the EMB.

observations.recommendation: Direct the LGU officials concerned to submit immediately the listed disbursement vouchers and its supporting documents shown in Annex A to avoid the issuance of the Notice of Suspension and Disallowance. Impose the applicable administrative and penal sanctions provided for under the cited regulations against the erring officials if warranted under circumstances.
observations.reason: Partial submission of DVs. Some vouchers were not found due to typhoon Yolanda and Ruby.

observations.recommendation: Require the former Mayor to immediately submit the lacking supporting documents to the cash advances to the Audit Team and Liquidation Report to the COA Chairman in a sealed envelope and the Transmittal Letter copy furnish the Audit Team. Henceforth, management should strictly follow the provisions of COA Circular No. 2003-003 dated July 30, 2003.
observations.reason: All accountable officers already dismissed from the service.

observations.recommendation: Instruct the designated Property Officer to render a Monthly Report of Fuel Consumption per vehicle for all vehicles. Strictly comply with requirements of COA Circular No. 75-6, dated November 7, 1975 and Section 4(2) of PD 1445.
observations.reason: Their validity, regularity, and propriety could not be ascertained.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Local Chief Executive or the Municipal Accountant direct the Municipal Accountant to properly record and reconcile the cash and cash equivalent following the prescribed accounting rules and Philippine Public Sector Accounting Standards (PPSAS) on the presentation of accounts in the financial statements in order not to mislead the user on the honest and reliable information on the financial position of the municipality.
observations.reason: Proper reconciliation and recording were not consistently performed to present accurate balances.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Local Chief Executive or the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Treasurer to deposit all the collections intact to the authorized depository bank, collection for the day should be deposited on the following banking day. Also, at the end of the day, require all the revenue collection clerks to remit all their collection to the treasurer to avoid exposure of funds to loss and misuse.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Mayor to submit the lacking supporting documents to the Audit Team and to strictly follow the proper granting, utilization and liquidation of cash advances, no additional cash advance shall be allowed to any official or employee unless the previous cash advance given to him is first settled and a proper accounting thereof is made. Henceforth, demand letters should be sent to officials and employees with outstanding balances to settle their cash advances.
