observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountant to strictly comply with the prescribed rules and regulations on the submission of financial statements. Likewise, it denied the management of timely and reliable information on the financial condition and performance of the agency for making decisions.
observations.reason: Financial statements and other reports or schedules are still not being submitted on time.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the municipal accountant should strictly follow the prescribed accounting rules and regulations and Philippine Public Sector Accounting Standards (PPSAS) on the presentation of accounts in the financial statements in order not to mislead the user on the honest and reliable information on the financial condition, performance, and equity of the municipality.
observations.reason: The municipality denied the LGU a timely and reliable information as required; accounts were not consistently prepared following PPSAS.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Local Chief Executive direct the municipal officials concerned to properly apportion an amount corresponding to the mandatory provision of 5% of IRA for LDRRMF. Direct the Municipal Accountant to thoroughly review the propriety of a transaction to be charged to the LDRRMF in accordance with Section 5 of NDRRMC-DBM-DILG JMC No. 2013-1. The MDRRM Officer, through the LDRRMC and LDC, to render a monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of LDRRMF as certified correct by the Municipal Accountant to the auditor and to post the same in a conspicuous place within the LGU quarterly.
observations.reason: The LGU did not fully comply with the required allocation and monitoring; some disbursements charged to LDRRMF were unrelated to disaster risk reduction activities.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Local Chief Executive direct the municipal official concerned to properly apportion an amount in the annual budget corresponding to one percent (1%) of the Internal Revenue Allotment (IRA) for Local Council for Protection of Children in order to support the implementation of its programs, projects, and activities.
observations.reason: No appropriation amounting to one percent (1%) was made for CY 2018.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Local School Board, headed by the Local Chief Executive, must follow the steps in the planning and approval of budget as specified by law and regulation in order that benefits be duly received by the intended beneficiaries. Also, prioritize related projects and activities chargeable to SEF and ensure proper documentation.
observations.reason: The SEF budget for CY 2018 is still not approved by the Local School Board. Despite non-approval, disbursements out of the fund were still made.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Local Mayor direct the municipal accountant to prepare and submit monthly bank reconciliation statements and maintain an updated related schedule for all funds maintained under the Cash in Bank account to present a reliable and accurate balances.
observations.reason: Bank reconciliation statements were not consistently prepared and bank statements were not submitted timely.

observations.recommendation: We recommend the Mayor to submit the said checks and disbursement vouchers as well as the original copies of cancelled checks in accordance with the provision of Sections 7.1 and 7.2 of COA Circular No. 2009-06.
observations.reason: Some accountable officers already dismissed from service; some checks and DVs not submitted.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Mayor direct the concerned officials to immediately submit the said disbursement vouchers and original cancelled checks in accordance with COA Circular No. 2009-06.
observations.reason: Some vouchers were submitted but others remained outstanding; some checks were cancelled and copies not provided.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Treasurer and concerned officials ensure compliance strictly with all the documentary requirements in the transfer, implementation and liquidation by the Implementing Agency (IA) and the Source Agency for proper verification and monitoring.
observations.reason: Management failed to present proof of submission of the lacking documents; subject to issuance of Notice of Suspension.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Treasurer ensure that all Fund Transfers are provided by a memorandum of transfer, Memorandum of Agreement for the intended purpose, and that such documents be submitted to the Auditor’s Office for review and verification.
observations.reason: No documents submitted in compliance with the audit observation; subject to further verification.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Mayor direct the MDRRMO concerned to strictly comply with Section 5.1.5 of COA Circular 2012-002 by submitting the Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF to the COA Auditor and post the monthly report in a conspicuous place within the LGU.
observations.reason: For CY 2018, the report was prepared and submitted upon demand by the audit team and monthly utilization report was prepared in prescribed format.

observations.recommendation: We recommend the Municipal Mayor to formulate and comply strictly with all the provisions of Republic Act No. 9003, specifically on the preparation and approval of a doable Local Solid Waste Management 10-year Plan and to re-submit the plan to EMB.
observations.reason: The LGU formulated and redrafted its Solid Waste Management Plan and submitted to EMB but the previously submitted plan was not approved.

observations.recommendation: We recommend calling a meeting with the Local School Board to prepare and approve an annual budget for SEF before expenses are made to serve as guide and to monitor priority projects and activities and to properly account actual receipts, disbursements and unexpended balance as of year-end.
observations.reason: Same audit observation in budget CY 2017; budget still not approved but disbursements were made.

observations.recommendation: We recommend the Local Chief Executive to submit the said disbursement vouchers and supporting documents to the Audit Team to avoid the issuance of the Notice of Suspension and Disallowance. Impose the applicable administrative and penal sanctions provided for under the cited regulations against the erring officials if warranted under the circumstances.
observations.reason: Some DVs were submitted; however, many were still missing due to typhoon Yolanda and Ruby; some supporting documents located, others not.

observations.recommendation: We recommend the Mayor to immediately submit the lacking supporting documents to the Audit Team and Liquidation Report to the Chairman. Henceforth, management should strictly follow the provisions of COA Circular No. 2003-003 dated July 30, 2003 and COA Circular No. 2012-001 for the proper utilization and liquidation of intelligence and confidential funds.
observations.reason: All accountable officers already dismissed from service; however, the Mayor was requested to furnish the Audit Team with the transmittal letter and liquidation report.

observations.recommendation: We recommend the concerned officials to comply with the liquidation requirements and submit proof of the transmittal letter or credit advice from COA Chairman and to ensure compliance with COA Circular No. 2003-003 dated July 30, 2003.
observations.reason: All accountable officers already dismissed from service; Mayor furnished partial documents; matter discussed with Chairman.

observations.recommendation: We recommend the concerned official, before vehicles are dispatched, Driver’s Trip Tickets are first duly accomplished and approved. Instruct the designated Property Officer to render a Monthly Report of Fuel Consumption per vehicle for all vehicles. Strictly comply with requirements of COA Circular No. 75-6, dated November 7, 1975 and Section 4(2) of PD 1445.
observations.reason: Trip tickets were not consistently accomplished; monthly fuel consumption reports were not rendered.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Treasurer be required to submit copies of Purchase Orders to the Auditor in accordance with Section 3.2 of COA Circular No. 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009 within five (5) working days from execution of contract and to ensure submission of supporting documents for audit review.
observations.reason: Some purchase orders were submitted; others remained outstanding; lack of consistent submission practices prevented timely audit review.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Mayor direct the Implementing Agency (IA) and the Source Agency (SA) to comply with Sections 3.1.1 to 3.1.3 of COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012 in order to achieve proper monitoring and evaluation of the intended projects, programs and activities charged to transferred funds.
observations.reason: Management failed to present proof of submission of the lacking documents. Subject to the issuance of Notice of Suspension.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Mayor ensure that all Fund Transfers are accompanied with the required Memorandum of Agreement or Trust Agreements and other supporting documents for validation and audit.
observations.reason: No documents submitted in compliance with the audit observation memorandum.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Local School Board, headed by the Local Chief Executive, prepare and approve an annual budget for SEF before expenses are made to serve as a guide and to monitor the priority projects, programs and activities to be undertaken and to properly account actual receipts, disbursements and unexpended balance as of year-end.
observations.managementAction: AAR, Call a meeting with the Local School Board as Co-Chairman of the board to prepare and approve a SEF representation with the Local School Board to approve an annual budget for SEF before expenses were made.
observations.reason: The budget was not approved by the Local School Board. Same audit observation in CY 2017.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that the Local Chief Executive should create an inventory
committee consisting of two or more employees including the property
custodian who will spearhead the physical count of property, plant and
equipment and inventories of supplies and materials, and submit the
Inventory Reports to the Commission on Audit for validation and
reconciliation to the financial statements in order to ascertain the fair
presentation of the accounts.

We also recommend that the Municipal Mayor directs the Municipal
Accountant to maintain Supply Ledger Card, Property, Plant and
Equipment Ledger Card, while the Supply Officer should also maintain
Stock Card and Property Card, he should also prepare and submit to
accounting unit the monthly Summary of Supplies and Materials Issued
(SSMI) based on the Requisition and Issue Slip (RIS).

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor directs the Municipal Accountant
to properly record and reconcile the cash and cash equivalent following the
prescribed accounting rules and Philippine Public Sector Accounting
Standards (PPSAS) on the presentation of accounts in the financial
statements in order not to mislead the user on the honest and reliable
information on the financial position of the municipality.

We recommend that the Local Chief Executive or the Municipal Mayor direct the
Municipal Treasurer to deposit all the collections intact to the authorized depository
bank, collection for the day should be deposited on the following banking day. Also,
at the end of the day, require all the revenue collection clerks to remit all their
collection to the treasurer to avoid exposure of funds to loss and misuse.

We recommend that the Local Chief Executive to direct the Municipal
Accountant to record the interest expense for the loan granted and strictly
follow the stated provision in preparation of the financial statements in order
to reflect the accurate and reliable information to achieve fair presentation of
the accounts presented.

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor directs the Municipal Accountant
concerned to strictly follow the proper granting, utilization and liquidation of
cash advances, no additional cash advances shall be allowed to any official or
employee unless the previous cash advance given to him is first settled or a
proper accounting thereof is made to avoid exposure of funds for misuse and
misappropriation and to ascertain the propriety and validity of the
transactions.

Likewise, demand letters should be sent to officials and employees with
outstanding balances to settle their cash advances.

We recommend that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal
Accountant to strictly comply with the prescribed rules and regulations on the
submission of financial statements. Likewise, it denied the LGU a timely and
reliable information on the financial condition and performance for decision
making.

We recommend that the municipal accountant should strictly follow the
prescribed accounting rules and regulations and Philippine Public Sector
Accounting Standards (PPSAS) on the presentation of accounts in the
financial statements in order not to mislead the user on the honest and
reliable information on the financial condition, performance, and equity of
the municipality.

We recommend that the LCE or the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal
Budget Officer, Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to thoroughly
review the propriety of a transaction to be charged to the LDRRMF in accordance
with Section 5 of NDRRMC-DBM-DILG JMC No. 2013-1, in order to prevent the
payment of unrelated expenses that could lead to disallowance in audit. All must be
guided by the LDRRMF Investment Plan in relation to disbursements out of the fund
in order that the purpose for which the fund was created be fully achieved.

Direct the MDRRM Officer, through the LDRRMC and LDC, to render a
monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of LDRRMF as certified correct by the
Municipal Accountant to the auditor, to facilitate review and monitoring of the fund.

Likewise, direct Municipal Accountant to monitor the submission of
Disbursement Vouchers and the immediate liquidation of cash advances to
ensure that all transactions are duly accounted and reviewed.

Furthermore, the Local Chief Executive thru the LDRRMC must initiate the
transfer of the unexpended balance of LDRRMF to a Special Trust Account in
accordance with the approved regulations pertaining to the utilization and reporting
of the fund.

This observation is without prejudice to the issuance of the Notice of Suspension
and/or Disallowance, if warranted.

We recommend that the Local Chief Executive direct the municipal officials
concerned to properly apportion an amount in the Annual Budget corresponding to
one percent (1%) of the Internal Revenue Allotment (IRA) for Local Council for
Protection of Children in order to support the proper implementation of its
programs, projects, and activities.

We recommend that the Local School Board, headed by the Local Chief
Executive, must follow the steps in the planning and approval of SEF budget as
specified by law and an approved regulation in order that benefits be duly received
by the intended beneficiaries. Also, the Municipal Mayor, Municipal Budget Officer,
Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer should approve payment of
transactions only if those are within the bounds of an approved budget to prevent
misuse of funds and a disallowance in audit. Furthermore, all concerned officials are
advised to check the documentary requirements of a transaction in compliance with
COA Circular 2012-001, before payment.

This observation is without prejudice to the issuance of the Notice of Suspension
and/or Disallowance, if warranted.

We recommend that the Local Chief Executive direct the municipal official
concerned to strictly comply with stated regulations in the preparation of the budget
and ensure that all projects, programs and activities identified for the Gender and
Development will promote equal gender opportunities in all spheres of public policies
and programs.
We recommend that the Municipal Accountant prepare and present monthly bank reconciliation statements and maintain an updated schedule for all funds maintained under the Cash in Bank account.
observations.reason: The Municipal Accountant failed to prepare the bank reconciliation for the Special Education Fund for CY 2017.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to immediately submit the said disbursement vouchers as well as the original cancelled checks in accordance with the provision of Sections 7.1 and 7.2 of COA Circular No. 2009-06.
observations.managementAction: AAR, Submitte five of the six of the six disbursement vouchers and its supporting documents.
observations.reason: The remaining lacking vouchers are not yet submitted.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Mayor direct the concerned to submit those disbursement vouchers and its supporting documents as the original cancelled checks in accordance with the provision of Sections 7.1 and 7.2 of COA Circular No. 2009-06 dated September 15, 2009.
observations.managementAction: AAR, Submitte five of the six of the six disbursement vouchers and its supporting documents as the original cancelled checks in accordance with the provision of Sections 7.1 and 7.2 of COA Circular No. 2009-06.
observations.reason: One voucher still not submitted. As for the cancelled checks, three (3) remain unsubmitted. Subject to the issuance of Notice of Suspension and/or Notice of Disallowance.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Mayor direct the concerned to strictly comply with Section 3 of COA Circular 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012 on the utilization of the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund to further strengthen the LGU’s capacity for Disaster Risk Reduction Management.
observations.reason: For CY 2017 there is still P22,000.00 unrelated personnel expense charged to the LDRRMF.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Mayor direct the concerned personnel to strictly comply with Section 5.1.5 of COA Circular 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012 and submit the Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF to the Auditor every 15th day of the following month.
observations.managementAction: AAR, LCE issued memorandum to submit the Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF.
observations.reason: For CY 2017, there was no regular submission of the Report. However, upon demand by the audit team, the MDRRMO prepared and submitted in 2018 the said monthly report utilization in its prescribed format.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Local Chief Executive should create a committee that will focus on the preparation and proper execution of a doable Local Solid Waste Management 10-Year Plan in compliance with RA 9003.
observations.managementAction: AAR, Create a technical group that will focus on the Local Solid Waste Management Plan.
observations.reason: Management will take charge of the listing of the properties of the LGU and started the listing of the properties damaged by Typhoon Yolanda. The Local Solid Waste Management Board was not provided to the audit team for the stated action plan.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Local Chief Executive should create a committee that will focus on the listing of the properties of the LGU and support them with all necessary documents and request relief from accountability as required per COA Circular No. 92-751 dated February 24, 1992.
observations.managementAction: AAR, Assign CY 2015 personnel who will take charge of the listing of the properties during Typhoon Yolanda and support them with all documents.
observations.reason: Documents are still lacking.

observations.recommendation: AAR, The Local Chief Executive should request from the Land Bank of the Philippines a schedule of amortization deducted from the IRA for the payment of the loan; reconcile records based on the schedule acquired; and prepare accounting entry to record payment of principal and the interest thereon to correct balances of the accounts in the Statement of Financial Position.
observations.managementAction: AAR, Request from the Land Bank of the Philippines a schedule of amortization deducted from the IRA for the payment of the loan; Reconcile records based on the schedule acquired; and Prepare accounting entry to record payment of principal and interest.
observations.reason: Implemented. The Municipal Accountant reconciled records and recorded amortization.

observations.recommendation: Management, specially the BAC, should plan all procurement on its Annual Procurement Plan and Annual Investment Plan.
observations.managementAction: AAR, Management, specially the BAC, should plan all procurement on its Annual Procurement Plan and Annual Investment Plan.
observations.reason: For CY 2017 procurement of materials and supplies were recorded in CY 2017.

observations.recommendation: Direct the LGU officials concerned to immediately submit those disbursement vouchers and its supporting documents for audit as shown in Annex A to avoid the issuance of the Notice of Suspension and/or Notice of Disallowance. Impose the applicable administrative and penal sanctions provided for under the cited regulations against the erring officials if warranted under the circumstances.
observations.managementAction: AAR, Initiate CY 2014 measures to submit disbursement vouchers and its supporting documents.
observations.reason: Some disbursements vouchers still not submitted. Subject to the issuance of Notice of Suspension and/or Notice of Disallowance.

observations.recommendation: Require the former Mayor to submit the supporting documents to the Audit Team and the cash advances to be liquidated. Henceforth, management should strictly follow the provisions of COA Circular No. 2012-001 and COA Circular No. 2003-003.
observations.managementAction: AAR, Initiate CY 2013 measures to require the former Mayor to submit the required supporting documents for liquidation and report to the COA Chairman.
observations.reason: All accountable officers already dismissed from the service. The former Mayor furnished the documentation to the Audit Team. Management committed to follow COA Circular provisions.

observations.recommendation: Comply with the requirements of COA Circular 2003-003 dated July 30, 2003 and furnish proof of the transmittal letter or the credit advice from COA Chairperson.
observations.managementAction: AAR, Initiate CY 2013 measures to require compliance with the credited advice and submission of proof.
observations.reason: All accountable officers already dismissed from the service. The former officers furnished documentation. Follow-up action by management is ongoing.

observations.recommendation: Strictly comply with the provisions of DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2010-83, as amended.
observations.managementAction: AAR, Implement CY 2012 provisions of DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2010-83.
observations.reason: LGU complied with the requirement and posted updated financial statements as amended.

observations.recommendation: Require that before vehicles are dispatched, the driver’s Trip Tickets are first properly accomplished and approved. The Property Officer to render a Monthly Report of Fuel Consumption of all vehicles. Strictly comply with the requirements of COA Circular No. 75-6 dated November 7, 1975.
observations.managementAction: AAR, Initiates CY 2012 contact concerned to render the monthly report and require proper trip tickets.
observations.reason: Some disbursements were not supported with trip tickets. Municipal Validation on the current year's practice and confer on the matter.

observations.recommendation: Drivers are required to submit duly accomplished Driver’s Trip Tickets, and to render a Monthly Report of Fuel Consumption. Strictly comply with the requirements of COA Circular No. 75-6.
observations.managementAction: AAR, Initiates CY 2011 measures to require submission of trip tickets and monthly fuel consumption reports.
observations.reason: Some disbursements are still unsupported due to absence of supporting schedule for Maintenance and Other Operating Expenses account.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Treasurer be required to submit copies of Purchase Orders to the Auditor in accordance with Section 3.2 of COA Circular No. 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009.
observations.managementAction: AAR, Implemented. The Treasurer submitted copies of Purchase Orders to the Auditor in accordance with Section 3.2 of COA Circular No. 2009-001.
observations.reason: Copies of Purchase Orders were submitted; prior deficiencies addressed.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Accountant prepare and submit monthly bank reconciliation statements and maintain an updated related schedule for all funds maintained under the Cash in Bank account to present a reliable and accurate balances.
We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer and all concerned LGU officials and employees submit the liquidation reports within the reglementary period.

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to immediately submit the said checks disbursement vouchers as well as the original copies of cancelled checks in accordance with the provision of Sections 7.1 and 7.2 of COA Circular No. 2009-06.

We recommend that the Municipal Accountant comply strictly all the documentary requirements in the transfer, implementation and liquidation by the Implementing Agency (IA) and the Source Agency (SA) pursuant to Sections 3.1.1 to 3.1.3 of COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012 in order to achieve properly and timely evaluation of the intended projects, programs and activities charged to transferred funds.
We recommend that the Municipal Accountant ensure that all Fund Transfers are provided by a Memorandum of Agreement for us to verify the intention/purpose of the said transfer.

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor direct personnel concerned to strictly comply with Section 3 of COA Circular 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012 on the utilization of the Local Disaster Risk Reduction Management Fund to further strengthen the LGU’s capacity for Disaster Risk Reduction Management. Such will likewise prevent the suspension or disallowance of pertinent transactions on future audits.

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor direct personnel concerned to strictly comply with Section 5.1.5 of COA Circular 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012 on the submission of the Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF to further strengthen the capabilities of the LGU in addressing Disaster Risk Reduction Management through regular monitoring of its PPAs.

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor through its Solid Waste Management Board comply strictly with all the provisions of Republic Act No. 9003 in order to achieve the intended purpose of the law, specifically on the preparation and proper execution of a doable Local Solid Waste Management 10-Year Plan.

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor as Co-Chairman of the Local School Board make a representation with the Local School Board to prepare and approve an annual budget for SEF before expenses were made to serve as a guide and to monitor the priority projects, programs and activities to be undertaken and to properly account actual receipts, disbursements and unexpended balance as of year-end.
